title: Paramecium cultures for RNAi
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1kq9m","text":"Filtering and preparing P. bursaria cultures for downstream RNAi","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":24,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Use an old culture of Paramecium bursaria (between one and six months old). # This is to ensure that cultures start from a vegetative growth stage

In a sterile flow hood, filter 100mL of P. bursaria culture through a 40um cell strainer (3x). Keep the flow-through. # This removes large clumps and debris from the culture # Remain in hood for the remainder of the protocol
Transfer P. bursaria to a fresh culture flask and leave for 1-2 hours. # This allows cells to digest any residual food present in the food vacuole
Aliquot 5uL onto a slide and count cells (x10). # This is to ensure the correct volume and cell number (~50 cells per well) is added
Pour P. bursaria into a sterile square petri dish. # Place the lid of the petri dish under one side so that the petri dish is at an angle

Using a multi-channel pipette, aliquot the calculated volume of P. bursaria into each well. # The final cell number should be ~50 cell per well # Shake the petri dish gently with your hand (two to three times), and then aliquot as quickly as possible # Try not to pipette back and forth in the petri dish, as this can push the cells to the edge and create an uneven distribution of cells across the wells

Top up to 250uL per well with NCL. # Autoclaved NCL with antibiotics added in the flow hood under sterile conditions: # Ampicillin ( 0.1 mg/mL ), IPTG ( 0.4 millimolar (mM) ), and β-sitosterol ( 0.0008 mg/mL )
Add a lid to the plate and place at 23 °C # Or whatever condition required for the experiment # Under standard conditions, ensure the plate is exposed to adequate light
creator: Ben Jenkins
